entry.title: Mass Extraction of FI Attachments: Automating Invoice Downloads from FB03/GOS and ArchiveLink(FIORI)
entry.content:
Introduction

In large-scale SAP ERP environments, financial auditors, shared service centers, and compliance teams frequently require bulk access to original invoice copies. Navigating through the standard FB03 (Display Document) transaction to manually download attachments—via either Generic Object Services (GOS) or ArchiveLink—is highly inefficient when dealing with hundreds or thousands of accounting documents.

This blog post introduces a custom ABAP utility, ZFI_INV_DOWN_ATTACH, designed to streamline and automate the mass downloading of vendor and customer invoice attachments directly to a local workstation or network directory.

Business Use Case & Functional Blueprint

1. The Core Challenge

Financial documents in SAP often store attachments in two entirely different repositories:

  1. GOS (Generic Object Services): Internal SAP database tables (via Knowledge Provider / KPro) typically used for ad-hoc uploads like Excel workbooks, Word docs, or random image files.

  2. ArchiveLink: External Content Servers (e.g., OpenText, SAP Content Server) where digitized or automated invoices (such as XSuite or XFlow inbound streams) are legally archived as PDFs.

Standard SAP provides no out-of-the-box mass export framework that bridges both storage layers simultaneously.

2. Functional Capabilities of the Utility

The tool provides high flexibility for business users through a dual-layered selection logic:

  • Flexible Input Methods: * Direct Selection: Query by specific accounting document numbers (BELNR), Company Code (BUKRS), and Fiscal Year (GJAHR).

    • Payment Run Integration: Query via Payment Run parameters (Run Date LAUFD and Identification LAUFI from table REGUP) to automatically pull all supporting invoices paid in a specific cycle.

  • Target Storage Disambiguation: Allows users to choose whether they want to extract GOS Attachments (Option R11) or ArchiveLink Business Documents (Option R21).

  • Automated Renaming Logic: Automatically maps abstract SAP document IDs to clean physical filenames formatted with the operational document numbers (e.g., [DocumentNumber]_[Index].PDF).

Technical Architecture & Deep Dive

The utility is structured around the classic structural lifecycle of an ABAP report: Dynamic Selection Screens -> Data Retrieval -> Link Resolution -> Binary Stream Transformation -> Presentation Layer GUI Download.

1. Dynamic Selection Screen Engine

To prevent user confusion, the screen toggles field visibility dynamically using the AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT event based on the selected execution pathway.

ABAP
 
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'RB2' AND r1 EQ 'X'.
      screen-active = '0'. " Hide Payment Run inputs if Direct Selection is active
      MODIFY SCREEN.
    ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'RB1' AND r2 EQ 'X'.
      screen-active = '0'. " Hide Direct inputs if Payment Run selection is active
      MODIFY SCREEN.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

2. Deep Dive: GOS Attachment Extraction (R11)

When extracting GOS data, the program targets the Relationship Table SRGBTBREL.

  • Object Identification: The unique instance ID (INSTID_A) is constructed using the concatenation of Company Code, Document Number, and Fiscal Year (BUKRS + BELNR + GJAHR).

  • API Extraction: It passes the relational document ID (INSTID_B) to the standard API SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1.

  • Hex Conversion: The raw content fetched into contents_hex is transformed via SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING and downloaded as binary streams (BIN) using GUI_DOWNLOAD.

3. Deep Dive: ArchiveLink & Content Server Extraction (R21)

For high-volume business documents archived externally, the architecture shifts to ArchiveLink tracking APIs:

  • Link Resolution: It calls ARCHIV_GET_CONNECTIONS matching object types BKPF or BUS2081 (Incoming Invoice) to locate pointers in the link tables (e.g., TOAV0).

  • Content Validation: It runs cross-checks against table TOASP to filter and guarantee that only text descriptions matching specific strings like *XFLOW INVOICE* are targeted.

  • API Delivery Pathways: The program attempts an export via the modern Object-Oriented Framework using cl_gos_attachments=>gc_cmd_export via an ArchiveLink item handler wrapper class. If blocked by network or GUI limitations (gv_html checking via ITS_PING), it elegantly drops back to standard HTTP pulling through SCMS_HTTP_GET_FILE, specifying the Content Repository ID (crep_id = 'Z1').

The Complete Source Code

Below is the complete executable implementation of the report.

*&--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report:         ZFI_INV_DOWN_ATTACH
*& Description:    Mass Download Framework for FB03 Attachments (GOS & ArchiveLink)
*&--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zfi_inv_down_attach.
TABLES : bkpf,regup.

TYPES : BEGIN OF gty_bkpf ,
          bukrs TYPE bkpf-bukrs,
          belnr TYPE bkpf-belnr,
          gjahr TYPE bkpf-gjahr,
        END OF gty_bkpf.
TYPES : BEGIN OF gty_output ,
          files TYPE string,
          bukrs TYPE bkpf-bukrs,
          gjahr TYPE bkpf-gjahr,
          belnr TYPE bkpf-belnr,
        END OF gty_output.

DATA: lt_bkpf    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF gty_bkpf,
      ls_bkpf    TYPE gty_bkpf,
      lt_output  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF gty_output,
      ls_output  TYPE gty_output,
      lv_file    TYPE char100.
DATA: o_alv      TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,
      o_cols     TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns,
      o_column   TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column,
      o_layout   TYPE REF TO cl_salv_layout,
      key        TYPE salv_s_layout_key,
      lx_msg     TYPE REF TO cx_salv_msg.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b5 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-s04.
  PARAMETERS : r11 RADIOBUTTON GROUP g11 USER-COMMAND cmd1 DEFAULT 'X',
               r21 RADIOBUTTON GROUP g11.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b5.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b4 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-s05.
  PARAMETERS : r1 RADIOBUTTON GROUP g1 USER-COMMAND cmd DEFAULT 'X',
               r2 RADIOBUTTON GROUP g1.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b4.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-s01.
  SELECT-OPTIONS: so_belnr FOR bkpf-belnr NO INTERVALS MODIF ID rb1.
  PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bkpf-bukrs MODIF ID rb1,
              p_gjahr TYPE bkpf-gjahr MODIF ID rb1.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b2 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-s02.
  PARAMETERS: p_laufd TYPE regup-laufd MODIF ID rb2,
              p_laufi TYPE regup-laufi MODIF ID rb2,
              p_xvorl TYPE regup-xvorl AS CHECKBOX MODIF ID rb2,
              p_zbukr TYPE regup-zbukr MODIF ID rb2.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b2.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b3 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-s03.
  PARAMETERS : p_file TYPE string.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b3.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'RB2' AND r1 EQ 'X'.
      screen-active = '0'.
      MODIFY SCREEN.
      CONTINUE.
    ELSEIF screen-group1 = 'RB1' AND r2 EQ 'X'.
      screen-active = '0'.
      MODIFY SCREEN.
      CONTINUE.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file.
  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>directory_browse
    CHANGING
      selected_folder      = p_file
    EXCEPTIONS
      cntl_error           = 1
      error_no_gui         = 2
      not_supported_by_gui = 3
      OTHERS               = 4.

START-OF-SELECTION .

  IF r1 EQ abap_true.
    IF so_belnr IS INITIAL OR p_bukrs IS INITIAL OR p_gjahr IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE 'Please enter all selection fields' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ELSEIF r2 EQ abap_true.
    IF p_laufd IS INITIAL OR p_laufi IS INITIAL OR p_zbukr IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE 'Please enter all payment run fields' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  IF r1 EQ abap_true.
    SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr
     FROM bkpf
     WHERE belnr IN @SO_belnr
       AND bukrs = @p_bukrs 
       AND gjahr = @p_gjahr
     INTO TABLE @LT_bkpf.
  ELSEIF r2 EQ abap_true.
    SELECT laufd, laufi, xvorl, zbukr, bukrs, belnr, gjahr
      FROM regup
      INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_regup)
      WHERE laufd = @p_laufd 
        AND laufi = @p_laufi 
        AND xvorl = @p_xvorl 
        AND zbukr = @p_zbukr.
        
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0 .
      SELECT bukrs, belnr, gjahr
        FROM bkpf
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_regup
        WHERE bukrs = @LT_regup-zbukr
          AND belnr = @LT_regup-belnr
          AND gjahr = @LT_regup-gjahr
        INTO TABLE @LT_bkpf.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
  
  IF lt_bkpf IS NOT INITIAL.
    SORT lt_bkpf BY belnr bukrs gjahr.
    DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_bkpf COMPARING ALL FIELDS.
    
    LOOP AT lt_bkpf INTO ls_bkpf.
      PERFORM download USING ls_bkpf.
    ENDLOOP.
    
    IF lt_output IS NOT INITIAL.
      TRY.
          cl_salv_table=>factory(
            IMPORTING r_salv_table = o_alv
            CHANGING t_table      = lt_output ).
        CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO lx_msg.
      ENDTRY.

      o_layout = o_alv->get_layout( ).
      o_layout->set_key( key ).
      o_layout->set_save_restriction( if_salv_c_layout=>restrict_none ).

      o_cols = o_alv->get_columns( ).
      TRY.
          o_column = o_cols->get_column( 'FILES' ).
          o_column->set_long_text( 'Downloaded Files' ).
          o_column->set_medium_text( 'Downloaded Files' ).
          o_column->set_short_text( 'Files' ).
          o_column->set_output_length( 50 ).
        CATCH cx_salv_not_found.
      ENDTRY.

      o_alv->get_functions( )->set_all( ).
      o_cols->set_optimize( 'X' ).
      o_alv->display( ).
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form download
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM download USING p_ls_bkpf TYPE gty_bkpf.
  DATA: ls_object        TYPE sibflporb,
        ls_object1       TYPE sibflporb,
        doc_id           TYPE sofolenti1-doc_id,
        lt_doc_id        TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF srgbtbrel,
        lt_objectheader  TYPE srm_t_solisti1,
        lt_objectcontent TYPE srm_t_solisti1,
        lt_content_hex   TYPE tabtype_solix,
        ls_doc_data      TYPE sofolenti1,
        lt_links_table   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF srgbtbrel,
        lv_length        TYPE i,
        lv_doc_id        TYPE sofolenti1-doc_id,
        lv_xstring       TYPE xstring,
        lv_filename      TYPE string,
        lvfile           TYPE string,
        lv_ext           TYPE char4,
        lv_index         TYPE char4,
        lt_connect       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF toav0,
        lv_mandt         TYPE sy-mandt,
        lv_arc_id        TYPE toaar-archiv_id,
        lv_path(1024)    TYPE c,
        lv_doc_id1(1024) TYPE c,
        lv_comp_id1(1024) TYPE c,
        lv_crep_id       TYPE scms_crep,
        l_key(1024)      TYPE c,
        lv_compid        TYPE string,
        lt_connections   TYPE TABLE OF toav0,
        lr_arl_item      TYPE REF TO zcl_arl_al_item,
        gv_html          TYPE crmt_boolean.

  CONSTANTS: cms_destination TYPE rfcdes-rfcdest VALUE 'SAPCMS'.
  CONSTANTS: lv_fil           TYPE char15 VALUE '*XFLOW INVOICE*'.

  ls_object-instid  = p_ls_bkpf-bukrs && p_ls_bkpf-belnr && p_ls_bkpf-gjahr.
  ls_object1-instid = p_ls_bkpf-belnr && p_ls_bkpf-gjahr.

  CALL FUNCTION 'ITS_PING'
    EXCEPTIONS
      its_not_available = 1
      OTHERS            = 2.
  gv_html = COND #( WHEN sy-subrc = 0 THEN abap_true ELSE abap_false ).

  IF gv_html EQ abap_false.
    CASE abap_true.
      WHEN r11.
        SELECT * FROM srgbtbrel INTO TABLE lt_links_table WHERE instid_a = ls_object-instid.
        IF sy-subrc EQ 0 .
          LOOP AT lt_links_table INTO DATA(ls_links).
            MOVE ls_links-instid_b TO doc_id.
            CLEAR :ls_doc_data,lt_content_hex,lt_objectcontent,lt_objectheader,lv_length.
            CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
              EXPORTING document_id = doc_id
              IMPORTING document_data = ls_doc_data
              TABLES object_header = lt_objectheader
                     object_content = lt_objectcontent
                     contents_hex   = lt_content_hex
              EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 4.

            TRANSLATE ls_doc_data-obj_descr TO UPPER CASE.
            IF ls_doc_data-obj_descr CP lv_fil.
              APPEND ls_links TO lt_doc_id.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.

          DATA(lv_lines) = lines( lt_doc_id ).
          DATA(lv_mult)  = COND #( WHEN lv_lines > 1 THEN abap_true ELSE abap_false ).
          
          LOOP AT lt_doc_id INTO DATA(lw_links).
            lv_index = |{ sy-tabix }|.
            MOVE lw_links-instid_b TO doc_id.
            CLEAR :ls_doc_data,lt_content_hex,lt_objectcontent,lt_objectheader,lv_length.
            CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
              EXPORTING document_id = doc_id
              IMPORTING document_data = ls_doc_data
              TABLES object_header = lt_objectheader
                     object_content = lt_objectcontent
                     contents_hex   = lt_content_hex
              EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 4.

            TRANSLATE ls_doc_data-obj_descr TO UPPER CASE.
            IF ls_doc_data-obj_descr CP lv_fil.
              lv_length = ls_doc_data-doc_size.
              IF lt_content_hex IS NOT INITIAL.
                CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
                  EXPORTING input_length = lv_length
                  IMPORTING buffer       = lv_xstring
                  TABLES binary_tab   = lt_content_hex
                  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 2.
              ENDIF.

              lv_ext = COND #( WHEN ls_doc_data-obj_type = 'XLS' THEN 'XLSX'
                               WHEN ls_doc_data-obj_type = 'DOC' THEN 'DOCX'
                               ELSE ls_doc_data-obj_type ).

              IF lv_mult = abap_true.
                CONDENSE lv_index.
                lv_filename = |{ p_ls_bkpf-belnr }_{ lv_index }|.
              ELSE.
                lv_filename = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
              ENDIF.
              
              lvfile = |{ p_file }\\{ lv_filename }.{ lv_ext }|.
              ls_output-files = |{ lv_filename }.{ lv_ext }|.
              ls_output-belnr = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
              ls_output-gjahr = p_ls_bkpf-gjahr.
              ls_output-bukrs = p_ls_bkpf-bukrs.
              
              CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
                EXPORTING filename = lvfile
                          filetype = 'BIN'
                TABLES data_tab = lt_content_hex.
              IF sy-subrc EQ 0 .
                APPEND ls_output TO lt_output.
                CLEAR ls_output.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDIF.
        
      WHEN r21 .
        REFRESH lt_connect .
        PERFORM get_links USING ls_object-instid ls_object1-instid CHANGING lt_connect.
        IF lt_connect IS NOT INITIAL.
          lt_connections = lt_connect.
          SORT lt_connect BY arc_doc_id.
          DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_connect COMPARING arc_doc_id.
          lv_lines = lines( lt_connect ).
          DATA(lv_mult_1) = COND #( WHEN lv_lines > 1 THEN abap_true ELSE abap_false ).
          
          LOOP AT lt_connect INTO DATA(ls_data).
            IF ls_data IS NOT INITIAL.
              lv_index = |{ sy-tabix }|.
              lv_arc_id = ls_data-archiv_id.
              lv_mandt = ls_data-mandt.
              lv_doc_id = ls_data-arc_doc_id.
              lv_compid = 'data'.
              
              IF lv_mult_1 = abap_true.
                CONDENSE lv_index.
                lv_filename = |{ p_ls_bkpf-belnr }_{ lv_index }|.
              ELSE.
                lv_filename = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
              ENDIF.
              
              PERFORM build_cache_key USING lv_mandt lv_arc_id lv_doc_id lv_compid CHANGING l_key.
              DATA(p_usage) = 'AL'.
              DATA(p_filename) = |{ p_file }\\{ lv_filename }.PDF|.
              
              ls_output-belnr = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
              ls_output-gjahr = p_ls_bkpf-gjahr.
              ls_output-bukrs = p_ls_bkpf-bukrs.
              ls_output-files = |{ lv_filename }.PDF|.
              
              DATA: p_message TYPE string.
              CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_CLTFC_FILE_GET' DESTINATION cms_destination
                EXPORTING usage    = p_usage
                          key      = l_key
                          filename = p_filename
                IMPORTING message  = p_message
                EXCEPTIONS OTHERS  = 2.
                
              IF p_message IS INITIAL.
                WAIT UP TO 1 SECONDS.
                APPEND ls_output TO lt_output.
                CLEAR ls_output.
              ELSE.
                lv_path = p_filename.
                lv_doc_id1 = lv_doc_id.
                lv_crep_id = 'Z1'.
                lv_comp_id1 = lv_compid.

                CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_HTTP_GET_FILE'
                  EXPORTING mandt   = sy-mandt
                            crep_id = lv_crep_id
                            doc_id  = lv_doc_id1
                            comp_id = lv_comp_id1
                            path    = lv_path
                            frontend = 'X'
                  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 10.
                IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
                  WAIT UP TO 1 SECONDS.
                  APPEND ls_output TO lt_output.
                  CLEAR ls_output.
                ENDIF.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDIF.
    ENDCASE.
    
  ELSEIF gv_html EQ abap_true. " Web/HTML Environment Path
    CASE abap_true.
      WHEN r11.
        SELECT * FROM srgbtbrel INTO TABLE lt_links_table WHERE instid_a = ls_object-instid.
        IF sy-subrc EQ 0 .
          LOOP AT lt_links_table INTO ls_links.
            MOVE ls_links-instid_b TO doc_id.
            CLEAR :ls_doc_data,lt_content_hex,lt_objectcontent,lt_objectheader,lv_length.
            CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
              EXPORTING document_id = doc_id
              IMPORTING document_data = ls_doc_data
              TABLES object_header = lt_objectheader
                     object_content = lt_objectcontent
                     contents_hex   = lt_content_hex
              EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 4.

            APPEND ls_links TO lt_doc_id.
          ENDLOOP.

          lv_lines = lines( lt_doc_id ).
          lv_mult = COND #( WHEN lv_lines > 1 THEN abap_true ELSE abap_false ).
          
          LOOP AT lt_doc_id INTO lw_links.
            lv_index = |{ sy-tabix }|.
            MOVE lw_links-instid_b TO doc_id.
            CLEAR :ls_doc_data,lt_content_hex,lt_objectcontent,lt_objectheader,lv_length.
            CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
              EXPORTING document_id = doc_id
              IMPORTING document_data = ls_doc_data
              TABLES object_header = lt_objectheader
                     object_content = lt_objectcontent
                     contents_hex   = lt_content_hex
              EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 4.

            lv_length = ls_doc_data-doc_size.
            IF lt_content_hex IS NOT INITIAL.
              CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
                EXPORTING input_length = lv_length
                IMPORTING buffer       = lv_xstring
                TABLES binary_tab   = lt_content_hex
                EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 2.
            ENDIF.

            lv_ext = COND #( WHEN ls_doc_data-obj_type = 'XLS' THEN 'XLSX'
                             WHEN ls_doc_data-obj_type = 'DOC' THEN 'DOCX'
                             ELSE ls_doc_data-obj_type ).

            lv_filename = |{ ls_object-instid }_{ ls_doc_data-obj_descr }|.

            IF lv_mult EQ abap_true.
              CONDENSE lv_index.
              lv_filename = |{ p_ls_bkpf-belnr }_{ lv_index }|.
            ELSE.
              lv_filename = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
            ENDIF.
            
            lvfile = |{ p_file }\\{ lv_filename }.{ lv_ext }|.
            ls_output-files = |{ lv_filename }.{ lv_ext }|.
            ls_output-belnr = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
            ls_output-gjahr = p_ls_bkpf-gjahr.
            ls_output-bukrs = p_ls_bkpf-bukrs.
            
            CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
              EXPORTING filename = lvfile
                        filetype = 'BIN'
              TABLES data_tab = lt_content_hex.
            IF sy-subrc EQ 0 .
              APPEND ls_output TO lt_output.
              CLEAR ls_output.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDIF.
        
      WHEN r21 .
        REFRESH lt_connect .
        PERFORM get_links USING ls_object-instid ls_object1-instid CHANGING lt_connect.
        IF lt_connect IS NOT INITIAL.
          lt_connections = lt_connect.
          SORT lt_connect BY arc_doc_id.
          DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_connect COMPARING arc_doc_id.
          lv_lines = lines( lt_connect ).
          IF lv_lines GT 1.
            lv_mult_1 = abap_true.
          ENDIF.
          LOOP AT lt_connect INTO ls_data.
            IF ls_data IS NOT INITIAL.
              lv_index = |{ sy-tabix }|.
              lv_arc_id = ls_data-archiv_id.
              lv_mandt = ls_data-mandt.
              lv_doc_id = ls_data-arc_doc_id.
              lv_compid = 'data'.
              IF lv_mult_1 EQ abap_true.
                CONDENSE lv_index.
                lv_filename = |{ p_ls_bkpf-belnr }_{ lv_index }|.
              ELSE.
                lv_filename = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
              ENDIF.
              
              PERFORM build_cache_key USING lv_mandt lv_arc_id lv_doc_id lv_compid CHANGING l_key.
              p_usage = 'AL'.
              p_filename = |{ p_file }\\{ lv_filename }.PDF|.
              ls_output-belnr = p_ls_bkpf-belnr.
              ls_output-gjahr = p_ls_bkpf-gjahr.
              ls_output-bukrs = p_ls_bkpf-bukrs.
              ls_output-files = |{ lv_filename }.PDF|.
              lv_file = ls_output-files.
              
              CLEAR lr_arl_item.
              CREATE OBJECT lr_arl_item EXPORTING is_connection = ls_data.
              TRY.
                  CALL METHOD lr_arl_item->copy_1_of_execute
                    EXPORTING ip_cmd    = cl_gos_attachments=>gc_cmd_export
                              file      = lv_file
                    IMPORTING ex_save   = DATA(lv_save)
                    RECEIVING rp_commit = DATA(lv_commit).
                CATCH cx_gos_al_no_authority cx_gos_al_not_supported cx_gos_al_error.
              ENDTRY.
              
              IF lv_save EQ abap_true.
                COMMIT WORK.
                WAIT UP TO 1 SECONDS.
                APPEND ls_output TO lt_output.
                CLEAR ls_output.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDIF.
    ENDCASE.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form build_cache_key
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM build_cache_key USING mandt TYPE sy-mandt arc_id TYPE csequence doc_id TYPE csequence comp_id TYPE csequence CHANGING key TYPE csequence.
  DATA: l_temp TYPE string, l_result TYPE string.

  l_temp   = escape( val = |{ sy-sysid }| format = cl_abap_format=>e_xss_ml ).
  l_result = l_temp.
  l_temp   = escape( val = |{ mandt }| format = cl_abap_format=>e_xss_ml ).
  l_result = |{ l_result }/{ l_temp }|.
  l_temp   = escape( val = |{ arc_id }| format = cl_abap_format=>e_xss_ml ).
  l_result = |{ l_result }/{ l_temp }|.
  l_temp   = escape( val = |{ doc_id }| format = cl_abap_format=>e_xss_ml ).
  l_result = |{ l_result }/{ l_temp }|.
  l_temp   = escape( val = |{ comp_id }| format = cl_abap_format=>e_xss_ml ).
  key      = |{ l_result }/{ l_temp }|.

  IF key <> l_result. " Truncation guard check
    CLEAR key.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form get_links
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_links USING p_instid TYPE sibfboriid p_instid1 TYPE sibfboriid CHANGING p_lt_connect TYPE toav0_t.
  DATA: lt_lporb     TYPE sibflporbt,
        go_badi      TYPE REF TO cl_ex_gos_mult_publish,
        gs_lporb     TYPE sibflporb,
        lt_connect   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF toav0,
        lp_object_id TYPE saeobjid,
        lp_objecttype TYPE saeanwdid.

  CONSTANTS: gc_srvc TYPE sgs_srvnam VALUE 'VIEW_ATTA'.
  CONSTANTS: lv_fil  TYPE char15 VALUE '*XFLOW INVOICE*'.

  CREATE OBJECT go_badi.
  gs_lporb = VALUE #( instid = p_instid typeid = 'BKPF' catid = 'BO' ).
  APPEND gs_lporb TO lt_lporb.
  
  CALL METHOD go_badi->if_ex_gos_mult_publish~add_objects
    EXPORTING flt_val  = gc_srvc
    CHANGING  ct_lporb = lt_lporb.

  IF lt_lporb IS INITIAL.
    gs_lporb = VALUE #( instid = p_instid typeid = 'BUS2081' catid = 'BO' ).
    APPEND gs_lporb TO lt_lporb.
    CALL METHOD go_badi->if_ex_gos_mult_publish~add_objects EXPORTING flt_val = gc_srvc CHANGING ct_lporb = lt_lporb.
  ENDIF.

  IF lt_lporb IS NOT INITIAL.
    SORT lt_lporb BY instid typeid catid.
    DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_lporb COMPARING instid typeid catid.
    LOOP AT lt_lporb INTO gs_lporb .
      lp_object_id  = gs_lporb-instid.
      lp_objecttype = gs_lporb-typeid.
      REFRESH lt_connect.
      
      CALL FUNCTION 'ARCHIV_GET_CONNECTIONS'
        EXPORTING objecttype  = lp_objecttype
                  object_id   = lp_object_id
        TABLES    connections = lt_connect
        EXCEPTIONS OTHERS     = 0.
        
      IF lt_connect IS NOT INITIAL.
        APPEND LINES OF lt_connect TO p_lt_connect.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDIF.

  IF p_lt_connect IS NOT INITIAL .
    SELECT * FROM toasp INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_toasp)
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @p_lt_connect
      WHERE ar_object = @p_lt_connect-ar_object AND language = @SY-langu.
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0 .
      LOOP AT lt_toasp INTO DATA(ls_).
        IF ls_-objecttext CP lv_fil OR ls_-objecttext CP 'xflow invoice'.
        ELSE.
          DELETE p_lt_connect WHERE ar_object EQ ls_-ar_object.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

 

Architecture Design Key Takeaways

 Modern Security Integration

Older download programs often utilized cl_http_utility=>escape_url to handle data assembly. This version uses the secure XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) protection library:

l_temp = escape( val = l_temp format = cl_abap_format=>e_xss_ml ).

Conclusion & Next Steps

This program is a plug-and-play solution for back-office process optimization. If you are preparing for an internal migration to an object-store model, or setting up automated validation bots for internal audits, utilizing standard API layers like ARCHIV_GET_CONNECTIONS alongside SALV reporting tools saves processing time and configuration overhead.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter June 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here a preview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Meet our Experts to Hear About Data & AI Content Updates

Join our session on June 9 to get the latest insights on:

  • SAP Datasphere: CQC Deployment Readiness and CQC Solution Optimization
  • SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-guided Implementation workshop series
  • SAP Business AI & Joule: Expert-guided Implementation workshop series

Stay tuned and join on YouTube

Successful Launch of Joule in SAP for Me

Joule in SAP for Me marks a major milestone in SAP’s journey to deliver a simpler, smarter, and more intuitive experience. It's more than a feature release — it is a transformation in how customers interact with SAP for Me. By leveraging conversational and agentic AI, Joule introduces a new way of working — one that prioritizes ease, speed, and personalization.

Learn more

How SAP Business Network Is Taking Shape on SAP BTP

Learn how SAP Business Network’s move to SAP Business Technology Platform affects data, integration, and day‑to‑day collaboration. It outlines what’s changing and why these updates matter for planning what comes next.

Learn more

Join Our Live Session: SAP Integration Suite, Enhanced Edition

entry.title: Where can I see SXI_MONITOR errors after successful retry?
entry.content:
In our ECC system we have orders going out via PI to a separate TM system. Normally they process immediately but for the example below in SXI_MONITOR there was a 3 minute delay:

And in the details I can see a retry count of 1 which likely means there was an initial error and then it automatically retried successfully:

Normally if I see an error that hasn't reprocessed I can see error information but for this one I can't find anything. Is there somewhere I can find this information?

entry.title: Re: Error Function code not defined for flow EWM RF Custom Application
entry.content:
I can't directly help, but I know that the answer will depend on your knowledge about making dynpros (screens) in general?
The PAI/PBO of a RF dynpro is probably the same as in a normal dynpro. Many years ago there was some special requirments because old Telnet RF terminals needed to use F-keys F1 and F4 in a different way than standard SAPgui, but today I think it it most common that the word "RF-terminal" is simply a rugged android device with a build in scanner and maybe with a pistol grip. Basically a mobile phone. These devices will run normal SAPGUI transactions via ITS Mobile ( or they will run Fiori apps or they will run RAP Apps or they will run CAP Apps or ... but that is probably not relevant with the RF Framework).

So it may depend on your setup and your hardware,  but I guess your apps must be with normal dynpros and normal PAI and PBO. 

If you don't know how to create aps with dynpros it will be difficult. When I leaned it I started with a 5 days course. I think it was around 1997. The course was called BC-410 and today it seems that someone has moved it to Youtube:

I don't have any real experience with with EWM in S/4 HANA, but I have made many RF transactions in SAP ECC and earlier versions.

In SAP ECC there are also a kind of a customizable RF framework like in EWM. These frameworks are very flexible to be able to handle all kind of requirements from customers around the world running many different RF devices with many different capabilities. This flexibility also makes it very very complicated. 

The flexibility is necessary for SAP to support all the relevant scenarios, but my experience from multiple customers is that each customer installation always decide for one scenario with one (or very few) types of RF devices and with one specific path thru alle the customizing.
In that case we have always chosen not to use the RF framework, but simple make normal transactions with dynpros and PAI and PBO - with all what is normally part of a SAPGUI tranaction. For the dynpros are then generated HTML templates. Finally the transactions are made available as web transactions via a ITS Based gui service in SICF (ITS Mobile).

I know that this may be a controversial approach and probably someone would want to raise a discussion about clean core. As I said I don't know EWM in S4/HANA, but I'm almost sure my approach will also work in a clean core EWM.

I f you want to stay with the framework and make your own apps, I suggest that you take a look on of the SAP standard transactions/programs/function modules in the solution and then make a Z program with same complexity to make the program work with the framework.

entry.title: UI5 Web Components Angular Wrappers Have a New Home in Fundamental NGX
entry.content:
The Angular wrapper library for UI5 Web Components has moved.

@ui5/webcomponents-ngx and @ui5/theming-ngx are now deprecated.
Their successor packages ship as part of Fundamental NGX

- @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents
- @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-fiori
- @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-ai

What's the benefit for you?

The new packages give you theming, content density, and i18n out of the box — without any extra wiring.

- Theme sync: the active SAP Horizon theme (light, dark, high contrast) propagates automatically to UI5 Web Components.
- Content density: compact/cozy mode is observed on every wrapper component - no separate setup.
- Language sync: the active NGX language syncs to UI5 Web Components.

Previously these required manual integration or were simply unavailable. They now come for free when you use the Fundamental NGX packages.

Is this a breaking change?

No API changes. Components, inputs, and outputs are identical. The migration is import-path replacement only.

How to migrate

See the full migration guide: https://community.sap.com/t5/frontend-ui5-sap-fiori-blog-posts/migrating-from-ui5-webcomponents-ngx-to-fundamental-ngx-ui5-webcomponents/ba-p/14413678 

The short version:

npm install @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents \
@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-fiori \
@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-ai \
@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-base

npm uninstall @ui5/webcomponents-ngx @ui5/theming-ngx

Then replace import paths (no template or input changes needed).

Questions or issues:

File them at: https://github.com/SAP/fundamental-ngx/issues

entry.title: Migrating from @ui5/webcomponents-ngx to @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents*
entry.content:
Effective: 2026-04-27 - @ui5/webcomponents-ngx and @ui5/theming-ngx are deprecated and no longer maintained.

Successor packages are part of the Fundamental NGX monorepo.

Package Mapping

Old package

New package

@ui5/webcomponents-ngx

@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents

@ui5/webcomponents-ngx (fiori sub-path)

@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-fiori

@ui5/webcomponents-ngx (AI sub-path)

@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-ai

@ui5/theming-ngx

Remove - replaced by Fundamental NGX theming (see below)

Components, Inputs, and Outputs

No API changes. Every component, input, and output is identical across the two libraries. The wrappers are auto-generated from the same UI5 Web Components schema. A find-and-replace of the import paths is all that is needed for component usage.

Import Path Rewrites

Before

// app.module.ts / app.config.ts
import { Ui5WebcomponentsModule } from '@ui5/webcomponents-ngx';
// or individual sub-paths:
import { ... } from '@ui5/webcomponents-ngx/main';
import { ... } from '@ui5/webcomponents-ngx/fiori';
import { ... } from '@ui5/webcomponents-ngx/ai';
// theming bootstrap (old)
import { Ui5ThemingModule } from '@ui5/theming-ngx';

bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, {
  providers: [
    importProvidersFrom(
      Ui5ThemingModule.forRoot({ defaultTheme: 'sap_horizon' })
    ),
  ],
});

After

// app.config.ts — component imports are per-package now
import { Button }    from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents/button';
import { ShellBar }  from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-fiori/shell-bar';
import { AIButton }  from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-ai/ai-button';
// Theming — bridges Fundamental NGX theming into UI5 Web Components automatically
import { provideUi5ThemingBridge } from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-base/theming-bridge';
import { provideUi5LanguageBridge } from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-base/i18n';
// Per-package theming service — inject to activate it (tree-shaking guard)
import { Ui5WebcomponentsMainThemingService }  from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents/theming';
import { Ui5WebcomponentsFioriThemingService } from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-fiori/theming';
import { Ui5WebcomponentsAiThemingService }    from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-ai/theming';

bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, {
  providers: [
    // ... your existing Fundamental NGX providers (provideTheming, etc.)
    provideUi5ThemingBridge(),   // syncs NGX theme → UI5 Web Components
    provideUi5LanguageBridge(),  // syncs NGX language → UI5 Web Components
    provideEnvironmentInitializer(() => inject(Ui5WebcomponentsMainThemingService)),
    provideEnvironmentInitializer(() => inject(Ui5WebcomponentsFioriThemingService)),
    provideEnvironmentInitializer(() => inject(Ui5WebcomponentsAiThemingService)),
  ],
});

Worked example - single component

Before:

// my-app/src/app/app.component.ts
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { Button } from '@ui5/webcomponents-ngx/main';

@Component({
  standalone: true,
  imports: [Button],
  template: `<ui5-button design="Emphasized">Save</ui5-button>`,
})
export class AppComponent {}

After (one import path change, nothing else):

// my-app/src/app/app.component.ts
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { Button } from '@fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents/button';  // ← only change

@Component({
  imports: [Button],
  template: `<ui5-button design="Emphasized">Save</ui5-button>`,
})
export class AppComponent {}

NGX APIs Now Available

Consumers of the new packages automatically gain access to Fundamental NGX cross-cutting features that were not available in the standalone @ui5/webcomponents-ngx library:

Feature

How it works

Theming

provideUi5ThemingBridge() syncs the active NGX theme (Horizon light/dark/HC) to UI5 Web Components automatically. No manual setTheme() calls needed.

Content density

ContentDensityObserver is injected into every wrapper component. Compact/cozy mode follows the NGX content density setting.

i18n / language

provideUi5LanguageBridge() syncs the active NGX language to UI5 Web Components.

Steps Summary

Install new packages:

npm install @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents \
             @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-fiori \
             @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-ai \
             @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-base

Uninstall old packages:

npm uninstall @ui5/webcomponents-ngx @ui5/theming-ngx

Find and replace import paths (no API changes):

    • @ui5/webcomponents-ngx/main → @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents/<component-name>
    • @ui5/webcomponents-ngx/fiori → @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-fiori/<component-name>
    • @ui5/webcomponents-ngx/ai → @fundamental-ngx/ui5-webcomponents-ai/<component-name>
    • @ui5/webcomponents-ngx (barrel) → individual sub-paths above

Replace theming bootstrap (see Import Path Rewrites → Theming above).

Build and verify. No template or input/output changes required.

Out of Scope

This guide covers migrating between the two wrapper libraries only.

  • No API changes to the new wrapper packages are planned as part of this migration.

Questions and Follow-up Issues

File issues at: https://github.com/SAP/fundamental-ngx/issues

entry.title: Re: SWAPS ST02
entry.content:
Hi

Could you please show us the actual Parameter set for following Parameters:

Catalog Buffer
rsdb/cua_buffer_size
rsdb/obj/buffersize

Program Buffer
abap/buffersize

Screen Buffer
rsdb/ntab/entrycount

Memory
em/initial_size_MB

Roll & Heap control
ztta/roll_extension
(If too low → heap explosion → swap risk)

abap/heap_area_dia
abap/heap_area_nondia
(Limits heap per work process
Too high = OS pressure
Too low = ABAP dumps)

Rgds
Dino

entry.title: Re: Composite Provider in Compatibility Mode (MPRO_COMPA_...
entry.content:
Hallo,

bei einer BW/4HANA Konvertierung eines MPROs in einen HCPR kann der HCPR im Compatibility Mode angelegt werden. Er ermöglicht dem konvertierten CompositeProvider, bestimmte Verhaltensweisen des ursprünglichen MultiProviders beizubehalten, hat  jedoch zwei Einschränkungen, siehe SAP Hinweis 2444718.

  • Join Conditions sind nicht erlaubt
  • Kann nicht in einen anderen HCPR verwendet werden

mit freundlichen Grüßen

entry.title: Re: Request for BADI to Enhance Inbound Delivery Validation
entry.content:
If I understand, you seek to implement validation to prevent duplicate vendor invoice numbers across inbound deliveries in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Constraints :

  • Standard functionality does not include out-of-the-box validation for duplicate External IDs
  • Custom development is subject to SAP's Cloud Clean Core principles
  • Potential Implementation Approaches:

BAdI Option :

  • Consider using BAdI BADI LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK (or similar) during inbound delivery creation

EWM Considerations :

  • Validation should occur before replication to EWM to prevent duplicate ASN numbers
  • Consider integration points between S/4HANA and EWM for consistent validation

entry.title: When the Supply Chain Breaks, Who Keeps the Fuel Flowing?
entry.content:
Sanctions imposed overnight. Critical infrastructure offline. Spot prices spike 15% before markets open. This is downstream operations in 2026, where competitive advantage belongs not to the largest operators, but to those with real-time decision intelligence. 

The oil and gas sector has always carried out operational complexity. What’s fundamentally different today is the simultaneity of disruption. Price volatility, geopolitical risk, regulatory intensification, and accelerated energy transition are no longer sequential challenges. They compound in parallel. 

Yet fuel must reach forecourts on schedule. Margin protection remains non-negotiable. End customers expect seamless service regardless of upstream disruption. 

This is the operational gap SAP’s Secondary Distribution Management (SDM) and Retail Fuel Network Operations (RFNO) were architected to address. 

The Challenge: Concurrent Disruption at Enterprise Scale 

Most fuel distribution operators maintain contingency plans for isolated incidents. The structural shift is toward layered, simultaneous events. A representative quarter now includes: 

Sanctions eliminate a long-standing supply relationship. Refinery outages tightening regional spot markets. EU sustainability disclosure obligations entering force. Retail margin erosion from competitors executing intraday pricing adjustments. 

Each scenario is manageable independently. Collectively, they expose every weakness in planning systems, logistics orchestration, and pricing execution. Market leaders aren’t deploying extraordinary tactics. They’re operating on SAP platforms designed for sub-24-hour response cycles instead of multi-day manual processes. 

SAP SDM: Maintaining Flow Under Constraint 

SAP Secondary Distribution Management (SDM)  handles the distribution layer by orchestrating product movement from supply points through depot networks to wholesale and commercial customers. It functions as the operational control plane for secondary logistics. 

Core Capabilities in Production Environments 

Supply corridor disruption response 
When sanctions, port closures, or infrastructure failures sever established routes, SDM doesn’t merely surface alerts. The system models alternative fulfillment scenarios in real time: which depots carry available capacity, which transport assets can be redeployed, and what cost delta results from activating secondary suppliers. These aren’t planning exercises. They’re operational decisions executed against live inventory positions and constraint-aware optimization logic. 

Continuous Product Replenishment 

Automates stock monitoring and replenishment for end-consumer sites using forecasting algorithms and ATG integration to predict delivery dates and trigger orders automatically, optimizing cycles and enhancing customer loyalty. 

Embedded compliance architecture 
Transport documentation, customs filing, and safety records generate natively within workflow orchestration. Compliance isn’t a downstream reconciliation task managed in disconnected systems. 

 

 

SAP RFNO: Margin Optimization at the Forecourt 

Where SDM ensures product reaches the right location, RFNO closes the margin loop once it arrives.

Every liter sold flows through automated settlement, with contract conditions applied, commissions calculated, and card transactions reconciled, without manual intervention or error. At network scale, that accuracy is where margin is preserved or lost.

Core Capabilities in Production Environments 

Network-wide operational visibility 
RFNO provides centralised visibility across every site: sales performance, inventory position, and stock variance, consolidated network-wide without relying on manual site-level reporting. When POS data is missing, incomplete, or outside expected parameters, the platform surfaces the exception immediately through automated error management, enabling the network team to act before settlement is impacted. 

Dynamic pricing execution 
This is where RFNO is designed to deliver measurable competitive advantage. On the roadmap, pricing strategies will integrate local competitive intelligence, commodity market movements, and margin targets, with AI-supported competitor price prediction and scenario-based pricing as part of the direction. In markets where crude benchmarks move multiple dollars intraday, the capability to adjust pump prices across hundreds of sites within hours rather than days is the differentiation RFNO is building toward.. 

Energy transition readiness 
Multi-fuel forecourts have transitioned from strategic planning to operational reality. Diesel, petrol, biofuels, HVO, EV charging, hydrogen: RFNO manages these as a unified operational framework, enabling infrastructure evolution without compromising current-period financial performance. 

Integrated Architecture: End-to-End Decision Intelligence 

The strategic value of SAP SDM and SAP RFNO isn’t delivered by either solution in isolation. It emerges from the closed-loop integration between them. 

When distribution systems maintain real-time awareness of retail network requirements, and retail operations signal demand pattern shifts back upstream, operators gain what most downstream enterprises still lack: a demand-sensing supply chain with sub-daily planning cycles. 

This translates to depot rationalization decisions grounded in actual retail consumption data, not spreadsheet projections. It enables promotional planning that factors in supply constraints. And it delivers faster, more informed responses when the next disruption materializes, because it will. 

Operational Scenarios: Real-World Execution 

Scenario 1: Supply route elimination 
Regional conflict triggers sanctions closing a primary import corridor. SDM roadmap response: within hours, volume allocation reassigns to alternate depot infrastructure, and fulfillment execution routes. Service levels hold. Customer operations remain unaffected. 

Scenario 2:  
Service Station Network Management

A network restructuring programme moves twelve stations from CoDo to CoCo over a six-month period. Each transition changes the settlement logic, the financial flows, and the dealer relationship simultaneously. In RFNO, a station's operating model and settlement behaviour are governed by master data, when status changes, PAT (Data Collation) automatically applies the correct settlement processes for that model from the next settlement cycle. No parallel systems. No manual process switching. No settlement errors during the transition window. The network changes. Operations continue without interruption. 
 

Scenario 3: Network rationalization 
Combined analytics from SDM and RFNO identify that three depot locations can consolidate without elevating stock-out risk. The fixed cost base reduces while service level agreements are maintained. 

Strategic Imperatives for 2026 and Beyond 

The oil, gas and energy sector will not simplify. Energy transition acceleration, regulatory expansion, and geopolitical fragmentation are structural market conditions, not cyclical phenomena. The relevant question isn’t whether disruptions will occur but whether your enterprise systems can absorb them without operational degradation. 

SDM and RFNO don’t eliminate market uncertainty. They provide operators with visibility, response velocity, and decision support to transform disruption from existential threat into manageable operational variable. 

In today’s market environment, that distinction separates leaders from laggards. 

About Implico:

Implico is an SAP-Branded software and consulting partner specialising in Oil, gas and BULK logistics automation within SAP-led supply chain environments. Implico provides SAP-certified solutions designed expressly to extend SAP Cloud ERP and SAP supply chain processes with industry-specific automation, delivered in line with SAP’s clean core principles. 

Implico works closely with SAP, system integrators and end customers to enhance SAP-led processes, minimise manual effort and support scalable, future-ready supply chain workflows.

entry.title: Re: KO020 "open down payments" blocks CLSD on investment...
entry.content: Hi Raymond, thanks for the pointer to KBA 2130927 and Note 599689 — appreciate you taking the time. I read through both. The cause (a residual special G/L / down payment request item for the vendor on this WBS) lines up with what I'm seeing. The catch is that the KBA is scoped to ECC / on-premise / S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, whereas I'm on S/4HANA Cloud **Public** Edition — so FBL1, F-44, SE16/BSIK and F110 aren't available to run the resolution directly. On the Public side I've already verified there's nothing open via Manage Supplier Line Items (incl. special G/L) and the down payment G/L balance is 0, so it looks like a residual record that the standard apps don't expose. Given SE16/F-44 aren't options here, I'll raise a support case to have the residual down payment record investigated. Thanks again for the steer — it confirmed where to look.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Failed Mail Trace in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi habeeb,

Short version: No, there is currently no standard application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that captures invalid or non-existent recipient email addresses, and the behaviour you observed is expected rather than a defect.

The reason is architectural. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, outbound email leaves the system through SAP's cloud email infrastructure. The status you see in Monitor Email Transmissions (and the Completed status on the output item) only confirms that the email was successfully handed over from SAP to that mail infrastructure - it does not confirm delivery to the recipient's mailbox. Because SAP has no visibility of what happens after handoff, it still reports Sent or Completed, even when the recipient's mail server later rejects or silently drops the message. This is why a deliberately wrong or non-existent address still shows Sent: the handover itself succeeded; the rejection happens downstream, outside SAP's scope.

On the specific question of bounce/non-existent-address detection: the bounce is not reflected back into the S/4HANA Cloud monitoring app; the bounce message is sent back to the sender's address (or the return-path configured in your mail server), so to monitor these you need to check your mail server logs or configure bounce-handling in your email infrastructure (e.g. Microsoft Exchange, an SMTP provider, or the SAP BTP mail service). In other words, capturing invalid/non-existent addresses is a customer-side mail-infrastructure task, not a function exposed inside the S/4HANA Cloud tenant.

What the in-system monitoring can surface is errors that occur before handover - for example a missing recipient address, or a sender/receiver domain that isn't allowed. These errors occur earlier in the process, where the system refuses to even attempt sending because of missing or misconfigured settings (e.g. the "no recipient email address" case, or sender/receiver domains maintained via SSCUI 101311 / 101312 / 101313). A syntactically valid but non-existent mailbox, however, passes those checks and is handed over normally.

Before concluding it's purely a downstream issue, it's worth confirming the two mandatory email-security settings, because a missing one produces the same "Sent but never arrives" symptom for valid addresses too:

  • The SPF record for the sender domain must be published
  • Mandatory DKIM must be set up.

If either is missing, recipient servers may silently discard the mail. See KBA 3274509 and KBA 3265907, plus the mandatory-DKIM KBA 3223182, and the Setting Up Output Management (1LQ) guide for the exact SPF/DKIM steps.

SAP Practical recommendations:

  • For proactive prevention, keep recipient email data clean in the business partner master data, since the system can only validate format, not existence.
  • For reactive detection of bounces/non-existent mailboxes, implement bounce handling on your own mail relay or mail platform and monitor its logs — that is the only place the NDR actually lands.

Other Reference Links:

  • Monitoring Email Transmissions
  • Troubleshooting Email Output

Hope it helps.

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Production order Operations scheduled on weekends
entry.content:
Hello @johnsplit123 

Check your factory calendar settings and verify that weekends are off there. If a calendar is set in the operation's work center, make sure this calendar defines weekends as non-working days. Otherwise, the factory calendar is used.

Consider notes:

  • 3740986 - Factory Calendar Not Considered with Scheduling Type “Only Capacity Requirements”
  • 150007 - Consulting: Scheduling of production orders

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Creating Standard Profit Center Hierarchy in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2602)
entry.content:
Introduction

Organizational hierarchies are the backbone of financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. A well-structured Profit Center Hierarchy and Cost Center Hierarchy enables management reporting, cost allocation, profitability analysis, and internal controlling across your enterprise.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release 2602, both hierarchies are maintained through SAP Fiori apps and Business Configuration (BC) activities — no more transaction codes like KCH5 or KSH1. There is no standard Migration Cockpit object available for migrating cost center or profit center hierarchies. This blog walks you through creating and maintaining both hierarchies from scratch, step by step.

Prerequisites

Before you begin, ensure the following:

Requirement

Detail

SAP Role

Business Configuration Expert or Finance Configuration Specialist

Controlling Area

Must be created and assigned to Company Code

Fiscal Year Variant

Assigned to Controlling Area

 

Note: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Standard Hierarchy name is set during initial configuration and cannot be changed after profit centers or cost centers are created under it. Plan carefully before proceeding.

Part 1: Creating the Standard Profit Center Hierarchy

What Is a Profit Center Hierarchy?

A Profit Center Hierarchy (also called the Standard Hierarchy for Profit Centers) is a tree structure that organizes all your profit centers into logical groups — by region, business unit, product line, or any dimension meaningful to your business. SAP requires every profit center to belong to the standard hierarchy.

Step 1: Open Business Configuration

  1. Log in to your SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Search for the app "Business Configuration" or navigate via: Fiori Launchpad > Finance > Business Configuration
  3. In the search box, type: "Profit Center"
  4. Select the activity: "Define Standard Hierarchy for Profit Centers"

Step 2: Set the Standard Hierarchy Name

  1. Click "Edit" on the configuration activity.
  2. In the field Standard Hierarchy, enter a meaningful name. Example: PC_STD_HIER — Use uppercase, no spaces, max 12 characters.
  3. Click "Save".

Best Practice: Use a naming convention that reflects your company. For example, <CompanyCode>_PC_H -> 1000_PC_H

Step 3: Access the Manage Profit Center Groups App

  1. From the Fiori Launchpad, search for "Manage Profit Center Groups".
  2. Alternatively navigate to: Finance > Controlling > Profit Center Accounting > Manage Profit Center Groups
  3. The app opens showing the root node of your standard hierarchy.

Step 4: Create Hierarchy Root Node

  1. The Root Node is automatically created using the Standard Hierarchy name you defined (e.g., PC_STD_HIER).
  2. Click on the root node to expand it.
  3. You will see it is empty — ready for child nodes.

Step 5: Create Hierarchy Nodes (Groups)

Hierarchy nodes act as folders that group related profit centers.

Example Hierarchy Structure:

PC_STD_HIER  (Root)
|-- PC_AMERICAS
|   |-- PC_USA
|   +-- PC_CANADA
|-- PC_EUROPE
|   |-- PC_GERMANY
|   +-- PC_UK
+-- PC_APAC
    |-- PC_INDIA
    +-- PC_AUSTRALIA

To create a node:

  1. Click the root node to select it.
  2. Click "Create Child Node" (or the "+" icon).
  3. Enter the required fields (see table below).
  4. Click "Save".
  5. Repeat for each top-level node (e.g., PC_EUROPE, PC_APAC).

Field

Example Value

Description

Profit Center Group

PC_AMERICAS

Unique node ID

Description

Americas Region

Business-friendly label

Controlling Area

A000

Your controlling area

 

To create sub-nodes: click on the parent node, then "Create Child Node", fill in details, and save.

Step 6: Create Profit Centers and Assign to Hierarchy

  1. Search for "Manage Profit Centers" on the Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Click "Create".
  3. Fill in the required fields (see table below).
  4. Click "Save".

Field

Example Value

Note

Profit Center

PC_USA_EAST

Unique ID, up to 10 chars

Description

USA East Coast

Meaningful name

Controlling Area

A000

Same as hierarchy

Hierarchy Node

PC_USA

Select from hierarchy

Person Responsible

[User ID]

Optional but recommended

Profit Center Type

1

e.g., 1 = Revenue

Valid From

01.01.2024

Start date

Valid To

31.12.9999

Open-ended

Company Code

1000

Assign company code

Step 7: Verify the Profit Center Hierarchy

  1. Open "Manage Profit Center Groups".
  2. Navigate through the tree to confirm all nodes and profit centers are correctly placed.
  3. Use the "Search" function to locate individual profit centers.
  4. Cross-check: every profit center must appear as a leaf node under the hierarchy.

entry.title: Re: Teaching AI Agents Best Practices: A Skills Workspace for CAP Development
entry.content:
Hi @StefanGraf 

Good question. Skills and ADRs operate at different layers but complement each other well:
- ADRs / architecture context constrain the solution space — "we chose X because Y." I keep these as project files (e.g. a simple CONTEXT.md) the agent reads at session start.
- Skills provide the execution playbook within those constraints — "given X, here's the one way to do it well."
Skills work regardless of the workflow — greenfield, brownfield, spec-driven or not. They're equally useful when building a new app from a spec as when adding a feature to an existing codebase.
Regarding a spec-driven approach in the SAP context SAP Joule Studio could be interesting (https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/joule-studio.html), which is heading in that direction — turning requirements into implementations with AI assistance. 
Best,
Daniel
